units.title: The Science Lesson (Starter)
units.topic: Revision: present/past tenses, school subjects, question forms
units.grammar: Revision of present simple, present continuous, past simple. / Question review.
key_phrases: What did you discover?
I was measuring the liquid.
Have you ever done an experiment?

units.title: Natural Disasters
units.topic: Weather events, past simple vs past continuous
units.grammar: While I was sleeping, the earthquake started. / When the volcano erupted, people were running.
key_phrases: The tsunami destroyed the village.
Were you scared?
What happened during the earthquake?

units.title: In the Rainforest
units.grammar: I've seen a jaguar. / They've visited the Amazon. / Have you ever been to...? / Present perfect for experience
key_phrases: The rainforest gives us oxygen.
Many animals are endangered.
We've never cut down a tree.

units.title: The Rock 'n' Roll Show
units.topic: Music genres, present perfect with for/since
units.grammar: I've played guitar for three years. / She's been a singer since 2015. / Present perfect + for/since
key_phrases: I've liked rock music since I was ten.
She's been in the band for two years.

units.title: Mythical Beasts (Review)
units.topic: Legends, reported speech intro, present perfect review
units.grammar: He said (that) the monster was real. / Introduction to reported speech. / Present perfect vs past simple.
key_phrases: They said the dragon lived in the cave.
I've read many legends.
What's your favourite myth?

units.grammar: If you mix flour and water, you get dough. / If you add sugar, it will be sweet. / First conditional
key_phrases: If you bake the cake too long, it'll burn.
What will happen if I add more salt?

units.grammar: If water gets hot, coral dies. / Zero conditional for facts. / Present simple in both clauses.
key_phrases: If the water is clean, the fish are healthy.
What happens if coral dies?

units.title: Free Time
units.grammar: You should exercise more. / I have to practice every day. / You mustn't give up.
key_phrases: What should I do in my free time?
You should try yoga.
I have to finish my homework first.
units.clil: Social Science: How people spend free time

units.grammar: If I had a million dollars, I would travel the world. / Second conditional (unreal present/future).
key_phrases: If I could go anywhere, I'd visit Japan.
What would you do if you were rich?

units.title: Mystery at Sea
units.grammar: When we arrived, the ship had already left. / Past perfect for earlier past events.
key_phrases: He had hidden the treasure before he died.
By the time they found the map, the pirates had escaped.
